description: Choropleth map displaying a section of Poland with Natura 2000 (N2K) sites and associated buffer zones. The map highlights detailed boundaries of specific N2K sites, identified by black outlines and codes: `PLH220052`, `PLH220005`, and `PLH220037`. These codes indicate sites located in Poland (PL). A continuous red outer line represents a buffer zone surrounding these Natura 2000 sites, consistent with the 2km buffer mentioned in the documentation. Additionally, an isolated, elongated red-outlined polygon is present within the larger buffered area, potentially indicating a corrected "wrongly clipped buffer" or specific feature. A red label `4109` is placed near a black-outlined feature in the upper right, likely denoting a habitat type or specific feature within the N2K network, as defined under the Habitats Directive. The map illustrates the geometric extent and refinement of N2K Area of Interest (AOI) boundaries and buffers, including corrections for missing buffers, wrongly clipped buffers, and gaps. No scale bar, compass, or explicit reference year is visible on the map; however, the surrounding context refers to the second production phase (2016) using end-2015 N2K Database, with updates to reference year 2018.
